clause_ref: NEC 2023 Article 210.8 — GFCI Protection of Personnel + Article 406.12 — Tamper-Resistant Receptacles
_title: NEC 2023 US Special Locations — GFCI Scope
_note: US NEC uses GFCI (Ground Fault Circuit Interrupter) for personnel protection in specific locations rather than the IEC/BS 7671 RCD + special-location-zone model. NEC 210.8 enumerates locations requiring GFCI receptacles or GFCI circuit protection. NEC 210.12 adds AFCI requirements. NEC 406.12 requires tamper-resistant receptacles in dwelling units.

210_8_A_2_garages_accessory.description: Garages and accessory buildings (not used for habitation)

210_8_A_4_crawl_spaces.description: Crawl spaces — at or below grade

210_8_A_6_kitchens.description: Kitchens — all 15A/20A 125V receptacles serving countertop surfaces + within 6 ft of sink

210_8_A_7_sinks.description: Sinks — receptacles within 6 ft of outside edge of sink

210_8_A_9_bathtubs_showers.description: Bathtubs and shower stalls — within 6 ft

210_12_A_dwelling_branch_circuits.description: All 120V 15A/20A branch circuits supplying outlets/devices in dwelling unit kitchens, family rooms, dining rooms, living rooms, parlors, libraries, dens, bedrooms, sunrooms, recreation rooms, closets, hallways, laundry areas, similar rooms or areas

210_12_B_kitchen_dining.description: Kitchen branch circuits — must be both AFCI AND GFCI per 210.8(A)(6)

tamper_resistant_receptacles_406_12._purpose: All 15A/20A 125V receptacles in dwelling units MUST be tamper-resistant per NEC 406.12

engine_lookup._purpose: Flat key/value lookup for runtime engine consumption
